compiled_state.title: [dso:code-reviewer-light]: Agent writes scalar score field -> record-review.sh rejects findings with schema mismatch
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Incident Overview
dso:code-reviewer-light writes a scalar 'score' field in reviewer-findings.json but record-review.sh requires a 'scores' object with 5 named dimensions (hygiene, design, maintainability, correctness, verification). This causes record-review.sh to exit 1 with 'ERROR: reviewer missing score dimension: hygiene'.

## Actual Behavior
Agent emits: {"score": 1, "findings": [...]}
record-review.sh expects: {"scores": {"hygiene": ..., "design": ..., ...}, "findings": [...]}

## Expected Behavior
dso:code-reviewer-light should write the scores object schema by default, matching the contract in record-review.sh.

## Workaround Applied
Include the full required JSON schema explicitly in every reviewer dispatch prompt. This adds boilerplate and is error-prone.

## Observed during
Epic a171-6c65 on 2026-04-12 — required re-dispatching the reviewer twice for two separate tasks.
